about.label: Identity
about.title: About
about.description: Systems architect specializing in AI infrastructure, multi-agent orchestration, and graph memory systems.
about.aboutDescription: Building the operational core of next-generation AI products, from architecture to execution.
about.narrative: This platform is my live architecture workspace: a place where AI systems, orchestration layers, and automation infrastructures are designed, tested, and operationalized. The goal is not to ship isolated demos, but to construct modular ecosystems that learn, adapt, and compound in value.

focusAreas.title: Systems Thinking

about.narrativePart2: I believe the next generation of digital systems will be defined by their ability to orchestrate intelligence — not just compute it. That means protocol-first design, composable architectures, and infrastructure that treats AI as an operational layer, not a feature.
about.domainDescription: AI Systems Architect & Automation Engineer. Building the operational core of next-generation intelligent products — from protocol design to production deployment.

mcp.title: MCP Ecosystem
mcp.subtitle: Protocol-first orchestration layer
mcp.description: The Model Context Protocol gateway that connects AI models, tools, and operational data streams into a unified orchestration fabric.
notes: Protocol-first design: all AI tool calls are MCP-standardized, making the system model-agnostic.
Bidirectional context: both tools and models can push/pull context through the same protocol.
The gateway maintains a registry of all available tools with capability metadata.
Failure isolation: tool errors are contained and surfaced without cascading to model context.

graphrag.title: GraphRAG
graphrag.subtitle: Graph memory & retrieval system
graphrag.description: A graph-native retrieval-augmented generation system that stores and queries knowledge as interconnected entities rather than flat vectors.
notes: Entities and relationships are first-class citizens — not just text chunks.
Multi-hop traversal enables reasoning chains that flat RAG cannot support.
Temporal context: knowledge nodes carry timestamps enabling recency-aware retrieval.
Graph structure survives session boundaries — the AI remembers across invocations.

agents.title: AI Agents
agents.subtitle: Multi-agent orchestration fabric
agents.description: Coordinated autonomous agents with defined roles, tool access, and inter-agent communication protocols for complex task execution.
notes: Each agent has a role definition, a tool manifest, and a context budget.
AURA arbitrates agent spawning based on task complexity and available resources.
Agents communicate through a shared message bus, not direct function calls.
Long-running agents checkpoint state to GraphRAG for resilient execution.

automation.title: Automation
automation.subtitle: Intelligent workflow infrastructure
automation.description: Event-driven automation pipelines that reduce operational friction and increase throughput across the entire system surface.
notes: Event-first architecture: every automation is triggered by a real operational event.
AI decision nodes embedded in workflows for dynamic branching logic.
All automation runs are logged with full input/output visibility.
Retry policies and dead-letter queues ensure no event is silently dropped.
